All parties submit to the court's tentative ruling. Matter is not argued and the Court's tentative ruling is adopted.

Defendant's motion to quash deposition subpoenas is continued to October 28, 2025.

Defendant's First Amended Cross-Complaint alleges that plaintiff's use of a storage area is a breach of the parties' CC&Rs and has or will lead to an increase in insurance rates. (Plaintiff's RJN, Ex. A [First Amended Cross-Complaint, pars. 17, 30].) Based on this theory of liability, plaintiff properly subpoenaed three of defendant's insurers who have documents reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (Code Civ. Proc., section 2017.010.) Plaintiff's subpoenas, however, are overbroad as they seek "[a]ll DOCUMENTS" and "[a]ll COMMUNICATIONS" relating to the policies. (Vardeh Decl., Exs. A-C.) The subpoenas are not limited in time and seek information protected by the attorney-client privilege and work product doctrine. On September 9, 2025, defendant noted these issues by letter. (Vardeh Declaration, Ex. D.) Plaintiff responded on September 10, 2025, and stated that she needed more time to respond to the letter and extended the time to file a motion to quash. (Grove Decl., pars. 6-7.) Defendant failed to meet and confer in good faith and filed this motion on September 10, 2025.

The court orders the parties to meet and confer. The parties should discuss limiting the subpoenas to documents regarding an increase or potential increase in insurance premiums, exclusion of attorney-client communications and work-product material, the propriety of a "first look" agreement, preparation of a privilege log, the amount of coverage and applicability of Civil Code section 5800, etc.

On October 20, 2025, each side may file a supplemental brief outlining what progress has been made and any outstanding issues. The court defers ruling on the sanctions issue to October 28, 2025.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Motion To Cancel Written Instrument And Application For Injunction Against 1810 Jackson Street
Off Calendar Oct-09-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. TONY FRANCOIS, Esq. of Briscoe Ivester & Bazel LLP (tel: 415-402-2700) for Defendant and Cross-complainant 1810 Jackson Street Homeowners Association.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing re: PLAINTIFF J. BONNEY's Motion To Cancel Written Instrument And Application For Injunction Against 1810 Jackson Street.

Matter not argued/contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as modified:

Plaintiff J. Dennis Bonney's motion to cancel a written instrument and application for an injunction is denied. Bonney must re-file the application if he wants an injunction.

Bonney has owned real property at 1810 Jackson Street, #1 in San Francisco since 2002. Bonney alleges that Defendant 1810 Jackson Street Homeowners Association (HOA) adopted an amendment to its Covenants, Conditions & Restrictions (CC&Rs) stating that Bonney owned Unit #1 and had exclusive rights to the storage space S-1. Bonney further alleges that the HOA adopted a second amendment to its CC&Rs, revoking Bonney's use of his storage space. Bonney now moves to cancel the HOA's second amendment and applies for an injunction against the HOA.

Civil Code 3412 allows a court to cancel a written instrument if a plaintiff proves that "(1) the instrument is void or voidable due to, for example, fraud; and (2) there is a reasonable apprehension of serious injury including pecuniary loss or the prejudicial alteration of one's position." (U.S. Bank National Assn. v. Naifeh (2016) 1 Cal.App.5th 767, 778.) But the Court is unaware of any authority that suggests that a court may entertain such an application upon a noticed motion before a trial on the facts, and Bonney cites no authority for this proposition. Several recent California Court of Appeal decisions analyzing Civil Code 3412 indicate that "cancellation" must be pled as a separate cause of action. (Lauckhart v. El Macero Homeowners Assn. (2023) 92 Cal.App.5th 889, 899; Weeden v. Hoffman (2021) 70 Cal.App.5th 269, 292; McMillin v. Eare (2021) 70 Cal.App.5th 893, 918; Meridian Financial Services, Inc. v. Phan (2021) 67 Cal.App.5th 657, 673-676; Robin v. Crowell (2020) 55 Cal.App.5th 727, 739-741; Thompson v. Ioane (2017) 11 Cal.App.5th 1180, 1193.) The FAC contains no separate cause of action for cancellation of a written instrument under Civil Code 3412, and regardless, providing relief to Bonney before a trial on the facts would be premature. The motion is denied.

As for an injunction, Bonney must show that (1) he is likely to prevail on the merits at trial, and (2) the balance of the harms weighs in his favor. (Integrated Dynamic Solutions, Inc. v. VitaVet Labs, Inc. (2016) 6 Cal.App.5th 1178, 1183.) But Bonney has provided no analysis on the second prong. The application is denied without prejudice to allow Bonney to provide a complete analysis and substantiate the application with competent evidence. Bonney must re-file the application if he wants an injunction.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-17-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT J. BONNEY's DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT.

J. Dennis Bonney's demurrer to 1810 Jackson Street Homeowners Association's (HOA) first amended cross-complaint (FACC) is overruled. This dispute regards a condominium storage area. Bonney demurs that the FACC is wholly barred because a 2018 "amendment to the [HOA] governing documents" is effective (Memo. 2:7-13), but the FACC does not mention the amendment until the fifth of its seven counts (and the former counts do not incorporate the latter). Moreover, the HOA is not required at the pleading stage to prove the 2018 amendment ineffective. (See id.) And if a writing is attached to a complaint, plaintiff's interpretation of it must be accepted as correct on demurrer. (Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239.)

Order is signed.
